message:
wa-nu4.3.2.11: CLI command contract tests (30 tests)

Adds comprehensive integration test suite validating human CLI command
contracts across status, events, accounts, audit, rules, export,
reservations, doctor, stop, and approve commands.

- 30 subprocess-based tests using assert_cmd against temp workspaces
- Pre-populated SQLite fixtures for deterministic assertions
- Cross-cutting no-ANSI guarantee tests for plain mode
- Cross-cutting JSON parseability tests for all JSON-mode commands
- Secret redaction verification on audit output
- Stable exit code checks for error paths
- All 30 contract tests + 14 docs smoke tests pass, clean clippy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -414,8 +414,8 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.1","title":"MCP API spec: tools/resources, schemas, error codes, versioning","description":"# Task: MCP API spec (tools/resources, schemas, errors)

## Goal
Write down a concrete MCP surface that is:
- stable (versioned)
- minimal (token-efficient)
- complete (covers required operations)
- aligned with `wa robot ...` semantics

## Deliverables
### Tool list (initial)
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- **`wa.reservations`** (list active reservations)
- **`wa.reserve`** (create reservation)
- **`wa.release`** (release reservation)

### Resource list (initial)
- `wa://panes`
- `wa://events`
- `wa://accounts`
- `wa://workflows`
- `wa://rules`
- **`wa://reservations`** (read-only view of active reservations)

### Schema requirements
- Every tool returns a stable JSON object with:
  - `ok: bool`
  - `data | error`
  - `elapsed_ms`
  - `version`
  - `now`
- Error codes are stable strings (prefix `WA-MCP-...`).

### Safety requirements
- Any tool that causes side effects must pass PolicyEngine.
- Resources are read-only snapshots.

## Testing
- The spec must be directly testable:
  - tool output schemas are validated (ideally by reusing robot schema definitions where applicable)
  - parity tests ensure MCP responses match robot/internal behavior
- The MCP tests bead (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`) should be able to reference this spec as the canonical contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A future contributor can implement the server from this spec without re-reading `PLAN.md`.
- The spec explicitly states:
  - tool names
  - required params
  - output schemas
  - error codes
- MCP parity tests and schema validations pass.


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.10: Robot JSON schemas: versioned envelope + per-command outputs, validated in tests ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1

Added 2 new error codes (WA-MCP-0011, WA-MCP-0012). Spec now has full parity with robot CLI — 21 tools, 6 resources, 12 error codes. Params match RobotWorkflowCommands/RobotRulesCommands/approval handlers exactly.","created_at":"2026-01-30T04:03:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","title":"Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring)","description":"# Task: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust)

## Goal
Add an MCP server entrypoint that can be enabled/disabled via feature flag and shares core logic with robot mode.

## Requirements
- Behind `--features mcp` (no extra deps for default build).
- Clean lifecycle:
  - init
  - register tools/resources
  - serve
  - shutdown
- Deterministic error handling + logging.

## Design notes
- Do not implement business logic in handlers; delegate to existing "robot" functions.
- Prefer a thin translation layer:
  - MCP params → internal args types
  - internal result → MCP result

## Deliverables
- `wa mcp serve` (or `wa serve-mcp`) command.
- Minimal server with one read-only tool (`wa.state`) wired end-to-end.

## Testing
- Build/feature tests:
  - `cargo check --features mcp` passes
- Integration tests (see `wa-nu4.3.1.5`):
  - server starts and responds to a basic tool call using fixtures/stubs
  - error mapping is stable (no panics)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo run --features mcp -- mcp serve` starts server without panicking.
- `wa.state` tool works end-to-end against a fixture/stub or a real WezTerm instance.
","notes":"Picked via bv --robot-next/triage on 2026-02-06; BoldSpring continuing implementation to unblock wa-nu4.3.1.3/.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldSpring","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:21:03.400524995Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:57:47.808174034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:57:47.808107971Z","close_reason":"Validated MCP skeleton: feature-gated checks pass for wa/wa-core with --features mcp; wa mcp serve starts cleanly and reports wa.state tool on stdio transport.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-mcp","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:36Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:36Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:36Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:36Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:36Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.3","title":"Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs","description":"# Task: Implement MCP tools (full set)

## Goal
Implement the full MCP tool set defined in `wa-nu4.3.1.1` by delegating to internal/robot APIs.

## Principle
**One source of truth**: MCP handlers must call the same internal functions used by CLI/robot mode.
No duplicated parsing/validation logic.

## Tool implementation checklist
For each tool:
- Validate inputs (types, required fields)
- Call internal function
- Map result into MCP response schema
- Ensure errors are stable (`WA-MCP-...`)

Tools:
- `wa.state`
- `wa.get_text`
- `wa.send`
- `wa.wait_for`
- `wa.search`
- `wa.events`
- `wa.workflow_run`
- `wa.accounts`
- `wa.accounts_refresh`
- `wa.rules_list`
- `wa.rules_test`
- **`wa.reservations`**
- **`wa.reserve`**
- **`wa.release`**

## Safety
- `wa.send`, `wa.workflow_run`, and `wa.reserve` must enforce PolicyEngine.
- Consider default-deny for any new tool that could mutate state.

## Testing
- Covered by MCP tests (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`):
  - schema stability
  - parity vs robot outputs
  - policy-gated tools denied when appropriate

## Deliverables
- Tool registry wiring for all tools.
- Consistent error codes and `ok/data/error` envelope.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each MCP tool can be invoked successfully with a trivial request.
- Tools that would act on panes are rejected when policy disallows.


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.7: [EPIC] Robot mode CLI (stable JSON envelope + core commands) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.1.5: Workflow runner: consume Detection events, select workflow, execute with policy gates ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.5: [EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.1.9: [Robot] `wa robot workflow` (run workflow by name, stable result schema) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.2.1.4: Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.5: MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.6: MCP audit integration: record each tool call decision/outcome (redacted) ● P2

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","title":"Implement MCP resources (wa://panes, events, accounts, workflows, rules)","description":"# Task: Implement MCP resources

## Goal
Expose read-only resources that mirror what a human would see in `wa status/events/accounts/...` but in a machine-friendly way.

## Resources
- `wa://panes` — current registry + inferred state
- `wa://events` — recent events (optionally unhandled)
- `wa://accounts` — known accounts + usage snapshot
- `wa://workflows` — available workflows + descriptions
- `wa://rules` — enabled packs + pattern list
- **`wa://reservations` — active pane reservations (owner/TTL/reason)**

## Requirements
- Resources must be **read-only**.
- Resources should be cheap to compute; prefer cached snapshots from the watcher.
- JSON output must be stable and versioned.

## Deliverables
- Resource handlers registered in MCP server.
- Pagination options where needed (events).

## Testing
- Covered by `wa-nu4.3.1.5`:
  - schema stability (versioned)
  - parity checks vs robot/human query results where applicable
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agent can browse resources without invoking tools.
- Output remains stable across versions (schema changes require version bump).


LABELS: area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.5: Persist events, agent_sessions, workflow_executions, workflow_step_log ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.4.1: Pane registry + discovery loop (wezterm list, fingerprinting, lifecycle) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.1.1: Workflow engine types: Workflow trait, StepResult, WaitCondition, WorkflowContext ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.5: [EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.2.1.4: Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.2: Implement MCP server skeleton (fastmcp_rust feature, lifecycle, wiring) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.1.5: MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs ● P2
","notes":"Started implementation: added MCP resource handlers in wa-core (wa://panes, wa://events with {limit} template, wa://accounts with {service} template, wa://workflows, wa://rules with {agent_type} template, wa://reservations with {pane_id} template), registered resources in build_server_with_db, and wired wa mcp serve to pass workspace db_path into build_server_with_db. Validation currently blocked by concurrent non-MCP compile errors in crates/wa-core/src/runtime.rs from in-progress backpressure changes.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldSpring","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:22:13.168686818Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:04:59.543511244Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-mcp","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.5","title":"MCP tests: schema stability + parity checks vs robot outputs","description":"# Task: MCP tests (schema stability + parity)

## Goal
Prevent MCP from drifting away from robot mode.

## Test strategy
- Unit tests:
  - tool param validation
  - error codes
  - stable JSON schema snapshots
- Parity tests:
  - for a fixed fixture input (pane list/text/events), compare:
    - `wa robot ...` output
    - MCP tool output
  - they may differ in wrapper fields but must match in `data`

## Coverage requirements
- All tools listed in `wa-nu4.3.1.1` must have at least one schema test.
- Side-effect tools (`wa.send`, `wa.workflow_run`, `wa.reserve`, `wa.release`) must have policy‑deny tests.
- Reservation tools/resources must have parity tests vs robot outputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI runs MCP tests behind `--features mcp`.
- Adding a new MCP tool requires adding at least one schema/parity test.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Include at least one intentionally-different wrapper field to ensure parity tests compare the intended subset (not whole JSON).
  - Ensure schema tests fail if a required field is removed or an error code changes.


LABELS: area-mcp, area-tests, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.3: Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs ● P1
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.8: Robot mode tests: envelope stability, error codes, command outputs (synthetic) ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.4: Implement MCP resources (wa://panes, events, accounts, workflows, rules) ● P2
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:22:27.775968512Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T15:48:10.401050908Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-mcp","area-tests","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

","notes":"Completed MCP resource implementation with base+templated resources and validated behavior:
- Registered base resources: wa://panes, wa://events, wa://accounts, wa://workflows, wa://rules, wa://reservations
- Added resource templates: wa://events/{limit}, wa://events/unhandled/{limit}, wa://accounts/{service}, wa://rules/{agent_type}, wa://reservations/{pane_id}
- Fixed FastMCP template registration gap by splitting base resources from templated handlers so base URIs remain discoverable
- Verified server startup now reports Resources: 6 (previously 2)
- Added automated registration tests in mcp.rs for with-db and without-db surfaces
Validation:
- cargo test -p wa-core --features mcp mcp_server_ -- --nocapture (pass)
- cargo check -p wa-core --features mcp (pass)
- cargo check -p wa --features mcp (pass)
- cargo clippy -p wa-core --features mcp -- -D warnings (pass)
- cargo clippy -p wa --features mcp -- -D warnings (pass)
- cargo run -p wa --features mcp -- mcp serve (smoke pass; Resources: 6)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldSpring","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:22:13.168686818Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:29:54.981364586Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:29:54.981219958Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-mcp","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}

    - `wa robot ...` output
    - MCP tool output
  - they may differ in wrapper fields but must match in `data`

## Coverage requirements

- Reservation tools/resources must have parity tests vs robot outputs.

## Acceptance Criteria

## Testing

  - Include at least one intentionally-different wrapper field to ensure parity tests compare the intended subset (not whole JSON).
  - Ensure schema tests fail if a required field is removed or an error code changes.


LABELS: area-mcp, area-tests, phase-4

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.1.6","title":"MCP audit integration: record each tool call decision/outcome (redacted)","description":"# Task: MCP audit integration

## Goal
Ensure MCP usage is trustworthy by recording an audit record for each MCP tool call.

MCP is high-risk because it is typically invoked by an agent. We need:
- a trace of what was requested
- why it was allowed/denied
- what was actually executed

## Requirements
- For each MCP tool invocation:
  - record tool name
  - record a redacted summary of arguments
  - record policy decision and preconditions
  - record outcome (success/error) and elapsed time
- Link audit entries to any underlying action audit entries (send/workflow).

## Redaction
- Do not store secrets or full inputs.
- Store hashes/summaries consistent with the shared redaction system.

## Testing
- MCP tests (`wa-nu4.3.1.5`) must cover:
  - tool call emits an audit record on allow AND deny
  - redaction behavior on representative arg payloads
  - linkage between MCP audit record and underlying action audit record (when applicable)
  - reservation tool calls (`wa.reserve`/`wa.release`) emit audit rows

## Acceptance Criteria
- Calling an MCP tool always results in an audit entry, even when denied.
- Audit entries are sufficient to debug a failed MCP automation without reproducing.


LABELS: area-audit, area-mcp, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.7: Audit trail emission: record allow/deny for every action (send, workflow steps, MCP) with redaction ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1: [EPIC] MCP server via fastmcp_rust (parity with robot mode) ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.1.3: Implement MCP tools (full set) by delegating to robot/core APIs ● P1

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2","title":"[EPIC] Human CLI UX: rich_rust tables/panels + ergonomic commands","description":"# [EPIC] Human CLI UX (rich_rust)

## Mission
Make wa pleasant and fast to use for humans while staying scriptable.

## Philosophy
- Default output is **human-friendly** when attached to a TTY.
- When piped (non-TTY), output becomes machine-friendly and stable.
- The same underlying data models feed:
  - `wa status/events/query/...` (human)
  - `wa robot ...` (machine)
  - MCP (machine)

## What this epic covers
- Rendering with `rich_rust` (tables, panels, highlighting)
- Command ergonomics (flags, defaults, exit codes)
- Clear error messages and remediation hints

## Testing & stability (non-negotiable)
This epic is “done” only if humans can trust the output contracts.

- Snapshot tests:
  - stable table/panel rendering in a non-ANSI test mode
  - stable ordering and truncation rules

- Contract tests:
  - each human command has deterministic exit codes
  - when non-TTY / `--format json`, output is machine-parseable and schema-stable

- E2E coverage:
  - the E2E harness must exercise the human surfaces for the most common workflows:
    - status/events triage
    - policy-gated send
    - running a workflow manually
    - accounts/rules diagnosis

(Concrete test beads in this epic: `wa-nu4.3.2.10` and `wa-nu4.3.2.11`.)

## Success Criteria
- A human can diagnose and fix a stuck agent using:
  - `wa status`
  - `wa events --unhandled`
  - `wa workflow handle_usage_limits <pane>`
  - `wa doctor`
without reading source.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All scope described in this bead is implemented without omission.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:22:47.167758456Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T02:17:17.239559924Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","title":"Output layer: rich_rust renderers + TTY auto-detect + stable plain output","description":"# Task: Output layer (rich_rust + TTY auto-detect)

## Goal
Create a small output/rendering module that all human CLI commands can use.

## Requirements
- Output format modes:
  - `auto` (default): rich if TTY, plain if not
  - `plain`: stable, no color
  - `json`: stable, machine-friendly (may share robot envelope)
- Ensure that piping does not emit ANSI escape sequences.

## CLI flag naming (avoid collisions)
- Use a single global output flag: `--format {auto|plain|json}`.
- Reserve `--output <path>` for commands that write files (exports, bundles), so `--output` is never ambiguous.

## Renderers to implement
- Pane state table (status)
- Event panel/list (events)
- Search results view (query)
- Workflow result summary

## Design
- Keep rendering separate from data acquisition.
- All renderers accept typed structs and return `String`.

## Testing
- Snapshot tests (see `wa-nu4.3.2.10`):
  - rich output normalized (strip timestamps/ANSI as needed)
  - plain output has zero ANSI escape sequences
  - json output validates against a documented schema/envelope

## Acceptance Criteria
- One new renderer can be added without changing command logic.
- Commands are consistent in how they print errors + hints.
","notes":"VERIFIED by claude-opus-4-5 (2026-01-21): All 15 output module tests pass. Implementation complete: OutputFormat enum (Auto/Plain/Json), TTY auto-detect, Style helper, Table formatter, and 4 renderers (PaneTableRenderer, EventListRenderer, SearchResultRenderer, WorkflowResultRenderer). Acceptance criteria met. Ready to close when parent epic permits.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenHarbor","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:23:03.460321082Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-25T04:45:56.374015620Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T04:45:56.373997005Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete and verified - output layer with rich rendering + TTY auto-detect","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-ux","impl-done","phase-4","verified"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:37Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":142,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification complete: Output layer fully implemented with format.rs (Auto/Plain/Json modes, TTY auto-detect, NO_COLOR/WA_OUTPUT_FORMAT env support), renderers.rs (PaneTableRenderer, EventListRenderer, SearchResultRenderer, WorkflowResultRenderer, RenderContext), table.rs (Column alignment, ANSI stripping, JSON rendering), Style helper for conditional ANSI formatting.
